title: persist(StorageLevel.NONE) on a Dataset gives wrong results
category: correctness (caching a relation at StorageLevel.NONE yields empty/incorrect query results)
crash_site: wrong query result (e.g. count() returns 0 / incorrect) after calling .persist(StorageLevel.NONE) on a Dataset; no exception, just a wrong answer downstream of the persist call.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: InMemoryRelation / the executed query plan that returns the wrong rows, and lookupCachedData. The defect is cacheQuery registering a cache entry for StorageLevel.NONE (which stores nothing) instead of not caching.
ground_truth.fix_summary: CacheManager.cacheQuery did not special-case StorageLevel.NONE; it registered an InMemoryRelation that caches no data, so later reads of the 'cached' plan returned nothing / wrong counts. Fix: do nothing in cacheQuery when storageLevel == StorageLevel.NONE (don't cache).

admission.note: Authored from PR #43188 (SPARK-45386) diff. crash != cause: symptom is a wrong count downstream; cause is cacheQuery caching a NONE-level relation (stores nothing) instead of skipping. Logic/correctness bug; somewhat greppable (StorageLevel.NONE) -> watch C2/C5. Scala is OK-tier in monogram, so this is a regression GUARD. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/apache/spark/pull/43188 — [SPARK-45386] Fix correctness issue with persist using StorageLevel.NONE; fix a0c9ab63f3bc, base 8f1b02880cb9.
